scenario: Recover a partially completed deployment workflow after a blocked dependency and continue with deterministic operational context.

tasks.title: Validate release contract
tasks.details: Check required artifact schema, benchmark name, and aggregate field names.

tasks.title: Resolve dependency lock mismatch
tasks.details: Pin benchmark code to standard-library modules and avoid adding package dependencies.

tasks.title: Document operational replay semantics
tasks.details: Explain why replaying operational state differs from storing full chat transcript history.

tasks.title: Prepare cloud-first review
tasks.details: Commit artifact, tests, fixtures, and benchmark documentation in one focused PR.

decisions: Model continuity as preservation of operational fields across compression and replay.
Use field-level survival rather than subjective quality scores.
Represent dependency relationships as sorted records with stable identifiers.
constraints: Do not introduce orchestration runtimes or planning engines.
Do not ingest PDFs or generate embeddings.
Keep benchmark data small enough for fast pull request review.
Use deterministic token counting based on regex word tokens.
failures: Dependency lock mismatch blocks adding third-party replay libraries.
Deployment checklist cannot proceed until artifact schema is stable.
recoveries: Use only Python dataclasses, json, math, pathlib, and re.
Encode compact replay state with abbreviated deterministic JSON keys.
Validate aggregate averages by recomputing from per-trace rows.

architecture_references.role: test-visible original, compact, and replay objects

deployment_requirements: Cloud CI must be able to regenerate artifact with a single Python command.
No benchmark output may depend on filesystem traversal order.
Reviewers must be able to inspect compact replay-safe representation.
unresolved_blockers: Deployment remains blocked until py_compile, pytest, and artifact generation pass.
Future traces need the same required operational fields to participate in aggregate metrics.

dependencies.detail: Dependency recovery depends on confirmed release contract.
Cloud review depends on benchmark rationale.

transcript_noise: Non-operational apologies, salutations, and cosmetic discussion are retained in the fixture only to prove extraction ignores transcript storage.
